summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships clipboard-hijacking cryptocurrency clipper with OS-level persistence
details: Package name `@jaggle/resizeobserves` is a typosquat of the popular `@juggle/resize-observer` (the README itself imports from `@juggle/resize-observer`). On `npm install`, `npm-install.js` installs a bundled Python daemon (`clipboard_guardian` / `guardian.py`) that polls the system clipboard and silently rewrites detected cryptocurrency addresses (BTC/ETH/Monero/Solana, etc.) to attacker-controlled wallets hardcoded in `MY_WALLETS` (e.g., ETH `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`) — `guardian.py:215` calls `clipboard_set(new_text)` after `replace_address_in_text`, directly stealing funds from any user who copies a crypto address on the installer's machine. The postinstall additionally establishes auto-start persistence under OS-impersonating names: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` on Linux, a LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist` on macOS, and a Scheduled Task `PyRuntimeBroker` on Windows; the daemon further sets its proctitle to `python3-dbus-helper` to disguise itself in process listings. None of this relates to the advertised ResizeObserver polyfill. The installer also performs privileged system mutation: `pip install --break-system-packages` against the bundled directory, and `apt-get/dnf/pacman install -y xclip` to guarantee clipboard access, with `SUDO_USER`/`sudo -u` handling to target the real user when run under sudo.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
